summary: sensivity@2.5.11 installs covert Windows Run-key persistence, evals an obfuscated bundle, and ships a game-memory cheat native module
details: On module load (launcher.js), the package writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run value named 'OneDriveUpdate' that points to a bundled VBS file 'OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs'. The VBS silently re-executes `node launcher.js` with a hidden window on every login (objShell.Run..., 0, False). The Run-key name and VBS filename are deliberately chosen to impersonate a Microsoft OneDrive component, and the running process sets process.title='Runtime Broker' to further masquerade as a Windows system process. launcher.js then reads server.obf.js (an 81KB heavily obfuscated bundle using a rotated string array and RC4-style decoder) and passes its full contents to eval() at top level. The deobfuscated code generates a hardware fingerprint by reading the Windows registry MachineGuid (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid), os.hostname(), and the C: volume serial via PowerShell, SHA-256-hashes them, and POSTs {key, hwid, nonce, app, version} to a hardcoded license endpoint hidden in the obfuscated string array. The package also ships a 6.8MB Windows native addon (sens.node, sha256 7a9ea06a85978c444f308639de2fc979239b26c378bd539ea58109f306908e77) containing strings 'Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', 'Settings' and is loaded by the obfuscated bundle as a 'cheat' module (cheatRunning, forte.startCheat) — game-process memory manipulation tooling. launcher.js additionally watches for Chrome windows whose title contains 'YouTube' to hide its UI, a streamer/anti-detection evasion pattern. Separately, `sens update` runs `taskkill /F /IM node.exe` and `taskkill /F /IM powershell.exe`, indiscriminately terminating every Node and PowerShell process on the host (IDE language servers, dev servers, build tools). Installer impact: covert autorun persistence under a deceptive Microsoft-branded name, unconsented host-uniquely-identifying fingerprint exfiltration to a hardcoded remote endpoint, opaque eval'd code on every boot, anti-cheat / anti-virus detonation risk and account bans from the bundled game-memory cheat, and disruptive process kills.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
